title: campylospermum calanthum
intro: Campylospermum calanthum, also known as the West African tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in West African medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Campylospermum calanthum include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a purgative and diuretic. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
